label: hate
rationale: 'Those murima slay queens will be the end of Sifuna' - negative attribution to Mt Kenya women as a group.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral analysis of Kikuyu voting patterns between candidates, no collective contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'They will be massacred post-2027' and 'They will see bad things here in front' - threat of ethnic violence.

label: neither
rationale: 'wantam people are insufferable' - unclear reference, no clear ethnic targeting.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Genzs are frauds, they lied' - insult to political/generational group, not protected characteristic.

label: hate
rationale: 'the payback will be very painful for Luos' - threatens ethnic revenge and wants dictatorial harm.

label: neither
rationale: Political prediction about opposition status conditional on behavior, not collective ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Accusing someone else of incitement, not itself inciting.

label: hate
rationale: 'Rent a Kikuyu' commodifies an ethnic group; 'we SHALL have post election violence in 2027' threatens violence.

label: neither
rationale: Defensive statement accusing others of targeting Kikuyus, not attacking a group.

label: neither
rationale: Reporting that Maasai people say they'll block roads, not attacking them as a group.

label: neither
rationale: 'Luos only had one father, rip jakom' - commentary on Luo political leadership, no contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kiluyus have never made anyone president in Kenya without rigging' - collective accusation of electoral fraud to entire ethnic group.

label: hate
rationale: 'our warlord refugee brothers with their guzzlers, flats, & attitudes to match' - stereotyping and negative attribution to national-origin group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'other tribe kingpin slaves' - insulting comparison of voting patterns between ethnic subgroups.

label: neither
rationale: Somali voting strategy discussion; 'Kikuyus can cry all they want' is dismissive but not hate.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wamunyoro reasoning virus is contagious' - disease metaphor attributes defective reasoning to ethnic group.

label: offensive
rationale: 'hawa wajinga wanaua wenzao wakisii' - criticizes Kisii intra-ethnic violence for money; 'wajinga' is insulting but targets behavior.

label: hate
rationale: 'white person... brain problems... behave like wild animals' - dehumanizing with collective negative attribution.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral analysis of Bukusu voting patterns as political punishment, no attack on group.

label: hate
rationale: 'you are a usurper cult... majosis' - religious hate against Shia with derogatory term.

label: hate
rationale: 'mluhya umpee tu unga anakupea votes' - reduces Luhya to being bought with flour; stereotypes both groups.

label: neither
rationale: Electoral coalition strategy suggesting Kikuyus sacrifice top seat, political discussion.

label: neither
rationale: Critical of Luo voting impact and political outcomes, not attacking group identity.

label: hate
rationale: 'Murima engages in politics of exclusion, vindictiveness, and hatred of other tribes' - collective negative attribution under NCIC standard.
